{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,

    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The draft makes causal claims (e.g., 'p95 latency by 19% because specialized pools converge faster') whose internal logic must hold under improvement; consistency between the classifier-adds-7ms claim and the latency gains also requires scrutiny.",
    "R1.3": "Specific quantitative claims (38% tail-round reduction, 19% p95 improvement, 22% GPU-hour saving) are present but unsupported by referenced tables or figures within the excerpt; alignment between these numbers and any underlying evidence is a live concern.",
    "R1.4": "The draft asserts 'first' three times in close succession ('first distributed serving system', 'first time … integrated into the serving control plane', 'first deployment-ready architecture'); verifying or qualifying these novelty claims is central to responsibly improving a research draft.",
    "R1.6": "The delegation 'Improve this draft' most directly invokes prose and sentence-level quality; this is the load-bearing interpretation and the default expectation for any draft-improvement task."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the improvement focus on prose and writing quality only (sentence clarity, flow, redundancy), or should it also include a technical review of the causal claims, quantitative evidence alignment, and the three 'first' novelty assertions?"
}

rationale.R1.2: The draft makes causal claims (e.g., 'p95 latency by 19% because specialized pools converge faster') whose internal logic must hold under improvement; consistency between the classifier-adds-7ms claim and the latency gains also requires scrutiny.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims (38% tail-round reduction, 19% p95 improvement, 22% GPU-hour saving) are present but unsupported by referenced tables or figures within the excerpt; alignment between these numbers and any underlying evidence is a live concern.
rationale.R1.4: The draft asserts 'first' three times in close succession ('first distributed serving system', 'first time … integrated into the serving control plane', 'first deployment-ready architecture'); verifying or qualifying these novelty claims is central to responsibly improving a research draft.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'Improve this draft' most directly invokes prose and sentence-level quality; this is the load-bearing interpretation and the default expectation for any draft-improvement task.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus on prose and writing quality only (sentence clarity, flow, redundancy), or should it also include a technical review of the causal claims, quantitative evidence alignment, and the three 'first' novelty assertions?
